PERFORMANCE COUNTS
The applica(cid:415)on on a shipping container roof with 1mm DFT, and The energy savings shown are very real, and unlike reflec(cid:415)ve
walls with 0.5mm DFT proved beyond a doubt to show the c o a(cid:415)ngs CharCoat CTI can work on the inside or the outside.
superiority of CharCoat CTI.
Iden(cid:415)cal containers along side each other. Iden(cid:415)cal A/C set to 22°C
(cooling only). Measured power consump(cid:415)on and temperature
(middle of roof externally and ~1.5m above ground in the center of
the container). A/C was maintained for one day, then no A/C for a
day.
The CharCoat CTI coated container was 14°C cooler internally
without AC. Coated container used <½ power to maintain target
temp, the uncoated could not maintain target. The coated container
barely went above ambient temperature even without AC. The
uncoated internal temp went higher than the coated external temp
without the AC. AC has to overcome much smaller temp gap for the
coated container. Change in temp vs. (cid:415)me also less for coated due to
the reduced heat flux density.

The CharCoat CTI coated container was 14°C cooler internally without AC. Coated container used <½ power to maintain target temp, the uncoated could not maintain target. (or even concrete) elements because it can impart significant forces
on structural members, fixings and their supports. For instance:
large bridge sec(cid:415)ons, railway lines, pipelines and large span or long
steel structures can all undergo significant stresses due to thermal
expansion/contrac(cid:415)on. Even when designed for the movement,

are res(cid:415)ng on supports can get worn down from the constant
abrasion against the supports.
CharCoat CTI insula(cid:415)on products can help to minimize the adverse
effects of thermal expansion/contrac(cid:415)on by minimizing the
temperature swings of the material.

For applica(cid:415)on at less than 90°C minimal dilu(cid:415)on is recommended
for the main coats. For applica(cid:415)ons above 90°C the product should
be diluted between 40-50% with potable water and applied in thin
layers un(cid:415)l the bubbling has stopped, then regular applica(cid:415)on can
proceed to desired thickness. Photos to the right show a 130°C
T-sec(cid:415)on during and a(cid:332)er applica(cid:415)on.
Filters should be removed from the gun and spray machine, and in
cases where the material may have formed a crust around the edges
of the bucket it should be strained through a filter sock to remove
any par(cid:415)cles that may clog the (cid:415)p. A 30 mesh machine filter can also
be used in some cases if the crusty material is minimal.
